content.content:
1	name: Code Quality
2	
3	# Tech-debt prevention gates that run on every PR to develop or master.
4	# Each lane is independent so failures are isolated and parallelised. The
5	# `quality-gate` job at the bottom is the single required check.
6	#
7	# Output convention for every step:
8	#   1. The step name is the plain-English explanation of WHAT is being checked.
9	#   2. The script body wraps the raw tool output in ::group::/::endgroup::
10	#      collapsibles so reviewers see a pass/fail summary line first.
11	#   3. The final echo is a one-line human + LLM readable verdict.
12	#
13	# Companion to .github/workflows/main.yml — that workflow handles
14	# correctness (build, type-check, tests). This workflow enforces code
15	# hygiene to prevent low-quality / LLM-generated code from accumulating.
16	on:
17	  pull_request:
18	    branches:
19	      - develop
20	      - master
21	  push:
22	    branches:
23	      - develop
24	      - master
25	  workflow_dispatch:
26	
27	concurrency:
28	  group: code-quality-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.head_ref || github.ref }}
29	  cancel-in-progress: true
30	
31	jobs:
32	  # --- Biome lint + format -------------------------------------------------
33	  biome:
34	    name: Biome lint + format
35	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
36	    timeout-minutes: 10
37	    steps:
38	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
39	        with:
40	          fetch-depth: 0
41	      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
42	      - name: Lint and format check across all TS/JS/JSON/CSS files
43	        run: |
44	          echo "::group::Biome check"
45	          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh ts tsx js jsx json jsonc css)
46	          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
47	            pnpm run quality:lint
48	          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
49	            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
50	          else
51	            pnpm exec biome check $FILES
52	          fi
53	          echo "::endgroup::"
54	          echo "Biome: OK (0 errors; warnings shown above are non-blocking)"
55	
56	  # --- Workspace dependency hygiene ---------------------------------------
57	  deps:
58	    name: Workspace dependency hygiene
59	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
60	    timeout-minutes: 5
61	    steps:
62	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
63	      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
64	      - name: All workspace packages agree on dep versions (syncpack)
65	        run: |
66	          echo "::group::syncpack lint"
67	          pnpm exec syncpack lint
68	          echo "::endgroup::"
69	          echo "syncpack: OK (dep versions and semver ranges consistent)"
70	      - name: Workspace topology is valid (manypkg)
71	        run: |
72	          echo "::group::manypkg check"
73	          pnpm exec manypkg check
74	          echo "::endgroup::"
75	          echo "manypkg: OK (workspace structure valid)"
76	      - name: package.json shape conforms (npm-package-json-lint)
77	        run: |
78	          echo "::group::package.json lint"
79	          pnpm run quality:pkg-json
80	          echo "::endgroup::"
81	          echo "pkg-json: OK (all package.json files conform to schema)"
82	
83	  # --- Circular dependency check ------------------------------------------
84	  circular:
85	    name: No circular imports
86	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
87	    timeout-minutes: 5
88	    steps:
89	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
90	      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
91	      - name: Circular dependency scan (madge)
92	        run: |
93	          echo "::group::madge --circular"
94	          pnpm run quality:circular
95	          echo "::endgroup::"
96	          echo "Circular deps: OK (no cycles found)"
97	
98	  # --- File size limit (per-extension + allowlist + hard cap) -------------
99	  file-size:
100	    name: File size limits
101	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
102	    timeout-minutes: 5
103	    steps:
104	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
105	        with:
106	          fetch-depth: 0
107	      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
108	      - name: Per-file line count (default 400, relaxed 700, hard cap 1200)
109	        run: |
110	          echo "::group::File size scan"
111	          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh ts tsx js jsx mjs cjs)
112	          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
113	            pnpm run quality:size
114	          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
115	            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
116	          else
117	            CHANGED_FILES="$FILES" node scripts/ci/check-file-sizes.mjs
118	          fi
119	          echo "::endgroup::"
120	          echo "File size: OK (no hard-cap violations)"
121	
122	  # --- Types must live in dedicated type files ----------------------------
123	  types-location:
124	    name: Types live in dedicated *.types.ts files
125	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
126	    timeout-minutes: 5
127	    steps:
128	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
129	        with:
130	          fetch-depth: 0
131	      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
132	      - name: Type / interface / enum location enforcer
133	        run: |
134	          echo "::group::types-location check"
135	          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh ts tsx)
136	          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
137	            node scripts/ci/check-types-location.mjs --enforce
138	          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
139	            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
140	          else
141	            CHANGED_FILES="$FILES" node scripts/ci/check-types-location.mjs --enforce
142	          fi
143	          echo "::endgroup::"
144	          echo "Type colocation: OK (no file exports > 3 types unless in *.types.ts)"
145	
146	  # --- Components-per-file ------------------------------------------------
147	  components-per-file:
148	    name: At most 2 React components per file
149	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
150	    timeout-minutes: 5
151	    steps:
152	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
153	        with:
154	          fetch-depth: 0
155	      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
156	      - name: Components-per-file enforcer
157	        run: |
158	          echo "::group::components-per-file check"
159	          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh tsx)
160	          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
161	            pnpm run quality:components
162	          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
163	            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
164	          else
165	            CHANGED_FILES="$FILES" node scripts/ci/check-components-per-file.mjs
166	          fi
167	          echo "::endgroup::"
168	          echo "Components: OK (no file declares > 2 React components)"
169	
170	  # --- Copy-paste detection -----------------------------------------------
171	  duplicates:
172	    name: Copy-paste detection (jscpd)
173	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
174	    timeout-minutes: 10
175	    steps:
176	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
177	      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
178	      - name: Duplicate code detection (threshold in .jscpd.json)
179	        run: |
180	          echo "::group::jscpd scan"
181	          pnpm run quality:dupe
182	          echo "::endgroup::"
183	          echo "Duplicates: OK (under threshold)"
184	
185	  # --- Type coverage ------------------------------------------------------
186	  type-coverage:
187	    name: Explicit-type coverage per workspace
188	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
189	    timeout-minutes: 15
190	    steps:
191	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
192	      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
193	      - name: Type-coverage (98-99% per workspace)
194	        run: |
195	          echo "::group::type-coverage per workspace"
196	          pnpm run quality:type-coverage
197	          echo "::endgroup::"
198	          echo "Type coverage: OK (every workspace meets its threshold)"
199	
200	  # --- Published package hygiene ------------------------------------------
201	  package-hygiene:
202	    name: Published package hygiene (publint + attw)
203	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
204	    timeout-minutes: 10
205	    steps:
206	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
207	      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
208	      - name: publint — validate packages/cli npm metadata
209	        run: |
210	          echo "::group::publint packages/cli"
211	          pnpm run quality:publint
212	          echo "::endgroup::"
213	          echo "publint: OK (packages/cli npm metadata is valid)"
214	      - name: are-the-types-wrong — validate type exports
215	        run: |
216	          echo "::group::attw packages/cli"
217	          OUTPUT=$(pnpm run quality:attw 2>&1)
218	          echo "$OUTPUT"
219	          if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "This package does not contain types"; then
220	            echo "::endgroup::"
221	            echo "attw: OK (packages/cli is a CLI binary — no exported types is expected)"
222	          else
223	            echo "::endgroup::"
224	            echo "attw: OK (type exports resolve across CJS/ESM/node16/bundler)"
225	          fi
226	
227	  # --- TypeScript strict type-check ---------------------------------------
228	  type-check:
229	    name: TypeScript strict type-check (all workspaces)
230	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
231	    timeout-minutes: 15
232	    steps:
233	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
234	        with:
235	          fetch-depth: 0
236	      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
237	      - name: tsc --noEmit across web, desktop, mobile, cli, bots
238	        run: |
239	          echo "::group::nx type-check"
240	          if [ -n "${GITHUB_BASE_REF:-}" ]; then
241	            git fetch --no-tags --depth=1 origin "$GITHUB_BASE_REF" 2>/dev/null || true
242	            pnpm exec nx affected -t type-check --base=origin/$GITHUB_BASE_REF
243	          else
244	            pnpm exec nx run-many -t type-check --projects=web,desktop,mobile,cli,bot-discord,bot-slack,bot-telegram,bot-whatsapp
245	          fi
246	          echo "::endgroup::"
247	          echo "TypeScript: OK (all affected workspaces pass strict tsc)"
248	
249	  # --- Python ruff --------------------------------------------------------
250	  python-ruff:
251	    name: Python ruff (strict rule set)
252	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
253	    timeout-minutes: 10
254	    steps:
255	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
256	        with:
257	          fetch-depth: 0
258	      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@d4b2f3b6ecc6e67c4457f6d3e41ec42d3d0fcb86 # v5.4.2
259	        with:
260	          version: "latest"
261	          enable-cache: true
262	      - name: ruff check + format (PL, RUF, FAST, S, B, A, COM, C4, DTZ, ISC, RET, SIM, ARG, PTH, TRY, PERF, UP, FURB, LOG, G + more)
263	        run: |
264	          echo "::group::ruff check + format --check"
265	          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh py)
266	          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
267	            uvx ruff check apps/api apps/voice-agent libs/shared/py
268	            uvx ruff format --check apps/api apps/voice-agent libs/shared/py
269	          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
270	            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
271	          else
272	            uvx ruff check $FILES
273	            uvx ruff format --check $FILES
274	          fi
275	          echo "::endgroup::"
276	          echo "ruff: OK (strict rule set passes; every file is already formatted)"
277	
278	  # --- Python mypy --------------------------------------------------------
279	  python-mypy:
280	    name: Python mypy (staged-strict)
281	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
282	    timeout-minutes: 20
283	    steps:
284	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
285	        with:
286	          fetch-depth: 0
287	      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@d4b2f3b6ecc6e67c4457f6d3e41ec42d3d0fcb86 # v5.4.2
288	        with:
289	          version: "latest"
290	          enable-cache: true
291	      - name: Install api dev + backend dependencies (uv sync)
292	        run: |
293	          echo "::group::uv sync"
294	          uv sync --project apps/api --group backend --group dev
295	          echo "::endgroup::"
296	          echo "deps: OK"
297	      - name: mypy across api + voice-agent + shared (staged-strict + shared.* strict override)
298	        run: |
299	          echo "::group::mypy"
300	          # mypy needs the whole module graph per project, so it can't be scoped
301	          # to individual changed files. On PRs we instead detect which Python
302	          # projects changed (from the .py diff) and check only those project
303	          # roots. libs/shared/py is a dependency of both apps, so any change
304	          # there pulls both apps in. On push we check everything.
305	          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh py)
306	          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
307	            uv run --project apps/api --group backend --group dev mypy apps/api/app apps/voice-agent/src libs/shared/py --ignore-missing-imports
308	          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
309	            echo "No changed Python files — skipping."
310	          else
311	            TARGETS=""
312	            if echo "$FILES" | grep -q '^libs/shared/py/'; then
313	              TARGETS="apps/api/app apps/voice-agent/src libs/shared/py"
314	            else
315	              echo "$FILES" | grep -q '^apps/api/'         && TARGETS="$TARGETS apps/api/app"
316	              echo "$FILES" | grep -q '^apps/voice-agent/' && TARGETS="$TARGETS apps/voice-agent/src"
317	            fi
318	            if [ -z "$TARGETS" ]; then
319	              echo "No tracked Python project changed — skipping."
320	            else
321	              uv run --project apps/api --group backend --group dev mypy $TARGETS --ignore-missing-imports
322	            fi
323	          fi
324	          echo "::endgroup::"
325	          echo "mypy: OK (no type errors)"
326	
327	  # --- Python docstring coverage ------------------------------------------
328	  python-interrogate:
329	    name: Python docstring coverage (interrogate ≥ 80%)
330	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
331	    timeout-minutes: 5
332	    steps:
333	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
334	        with:
335	          fetch-depth: 0
336	      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@d4b2f3b6ecc6e67c4457f6d3e41ec42d3d0fcb86 # v5.4.2
337	        with:
338	          version: "latest"
339	          enable-cache: true
340	      - name: Docstring coverage on public API surface
341	        run: |
342	          echo "::group::interrogate"
343	          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh py)
344	          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
345	            uvx interrogate -c pyproject.toml apps/api apps/voice-agent libs/shared/py
346	          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
347	            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
348	          else
349	            uvx interrogate -c pyproject.toml $FILES
350	          fi
351	          echo "::endgroup::"
352	          echo "Docstrings: OK (coverage ≥ 80%)"
353	
354	  # --- Python cyclomatic complexity ---------------------------------------
355	  python-xenon:
356	    name: Python cyclomatic complexity (xenon)
357	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
358	    timeout-minutes: 5
359	    steps:
360	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
361	        with:
362	          fetch-depth: 0
363	      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@d4b2f3b6ecc6e67c4457f6d3e41ec42d3d0fcb86 # v5.4.2
364	        with:
365	          version: "latest"
366	          enable-cache: true
367	      - name: Complexity scan (max-absolute F, max-modules E, max-average B)
368	        run: |
369	          echo "::group::xenon"
370	          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh py)
371	          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
372	            uvx xenon \
373	              --max-absolute F \
374	              --max-modules E \
375	              --max-average B \
376	              apps/api apps/voice-agent libs/shared/py \
377	              -e 'apps/api/tests/**' \
378	              -e 'apps/api/scripts/**' \
379	              -e 'libs/shared/py/tests/**'
380	          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
381	            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
382	          else
383	            uvx xenon \
384	              --max-absolute F \
385	              --max-modules E \
386	              --max-average B \
387	              $FILES
388	          fi
389	          echo "::endgroup::"
390	          echo "Complexity: OK (no module exceeds rank D, codebase average ≤ B)"
391	
392	  # --- Python security (bandit gates; pip-audit advisory) -----------------
393	  python-security:
394	    name: Python security (bandit; pip-audit advisory)
395	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
396	    timeout-minutes: 10
397	    steps:
398	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
399	        with:
400	          fetch-depth: 0
401	      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@d4b2f3b6ecc6e67c4457f6d3e41ec42d3d0fcb86 # v5.4.2
402	        with:
403	          version: "latest"
404	          enable-cache: true
405	      - name: Bandit security scan (zero-warning policy)
406	        run: |
407	          echo "::group::bandit (low severity, low confidence — no exclusions)"
408	          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh py)
409	          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
410	            uvx bandit -c pyproject.toml -r apps/api/app apps/voice-agent/src libs/shared/py --severity-level low --confidence-level low
411	          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
412	            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
413	          else
414	            uvx bandit -c pyproject.toml $FILES --severity-level low --confidence-level low
415	          fi
416	          echo "::endgroup::"
417	          echo "Security: OK (0 issues across all severity levels)"
418	      - name: pip-audit (dependency CVEs)
419	        continue-on-error: true
420	        run: |
421	          echo "::group::pip-audit dependency scan"
422	          uvx pip-audit --strict --skip-editable || true
423	          echo "::endgroup::"
424	          echo "pip-audit: informational — review CVEs above (does not fail CI)"
425	
426	  # --- Dead code detection (knip + vulture) — enforced ---------------------
427	  # Runs in --strict mode: any knip (TS) or vulture (Python) finding fails the
428	  # lane. The scan is tightened to drop systematic false positives (tests,
429	  # Pydantic/dynamic-dispatch, framework-consumed files) — see knip.config.ts
430	  # and apps/api/scripts/vulture-whitelist.py.
431	  dead-code:
432	    name: Dead code (knip + vulture, strict)
433	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
434	    timeout-minutes: 10
435	    steps:
436	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
437	      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
438	      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@d4b2f3b6ecc6e67c4457f6d3e41ec42d3d0fcb86 # v5.4.2
439	        with:
440	          version: "latest"
441	          enable-cache: true
442	      - name: Install vulture (Python dead-code detector)
443	        run: |
444	          echo "::group::uv tool install vulture"
445	          uv tool install vulture
446	          echo "::endgroup::"
447	      - name: knip (TypeScript) + vulture (Python) dead-code scan (strict)
448	        run: |
449	          echo "::group::dead-code scan"
450	          bash scripts/dead-code-check.sh --strict --verbose
451	          echo "::endgroup::"
452	          echo "Dead code: OK (no unused TS or Python code)"
453	
454	  # --- Quality gate (single required status check) -------------------------
455	  quality-gate:
456	    name: Quality gate (required)
457	    if: always()
458	    needs:
459	      - biome
460	      - deps
461	      - circular
462	      - file-size
463	      - types-location
464	      - components-per-file
465	      - duplicates
466	      - type-coverage
467	      - package-hygiene
468	      - type-check
469	      - python-ruff
470	      - python-mypy
471	      - python-interrogate
472	      - python-xenon
473	      - python-security
474	      - dead-code
475	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
476	    timeout-minutes: 2
477	    steps:
478	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
479	      - name: Verdict
480	        env:
481	          # Lane results passed through env to avoid inline-expansion injection.
482	          BIOME: ${{ needs.biome.result }}
483	          DEPS: ${{ needs.deps.result }}
484	          CIRCULAR: ${{ needs.circular.result }}
485	          FILE_SIZE: ${{ needs.file-size.result }}
486	          TYPES_LOCATION: ${{ needs.types-location.result }}
487	          COMPONENTS_PER_FILE: ${{ needs.components-per-file.result }}
488	          DUPLICATES: ${{ needs.duplicates.result }}
489	          TYPE_COVERAGE: ${{ needs.type-coverage.result }}
490	          PACKAGE_HYGIENE: ${{ needs.package-hygiene.result }}
491	          TYPE_CHECK: ${{ needs.type-check.result }}
492	          PYTHON_RUFF: ${{ needs.python-ruff.result }}
493	          PYTHON_MYPY: ${{ needs.python-mypy.result }}
494	          PYTHON_INTERROGATE: ${{ needs.python-interrogate.result }}
495	          PYTHON_XENON: ${{ needs.python-xenon.result }}
496	          PYTHON_SECURITY: ${{ needs.python-security.result }}
497	          DEAD_CODE: ${{ needs.dead-code.result }}
498	        run: |
499	          declare -A RESULT=(
500	            [biome]="$BIOME"
501	            [deps]="$DEPS"
502	            [circular]="$CIRCULAR"
503	            [file-size]="$FILE_SIZE"
504	            [types-location]="$TYPES_LOCATION"
505	            [components-per-file]="$COMPONENTS_PER_FILE"
506	            [duplicates]="$DUPLICATES"
507	            [type-coverage]="$TYPE_COVERAGE"
508	            [package-hygiene]="$PACKAGE_HYGIENE"
509	            [type-check]="$TYPE_CHECK"
510	            [python-ruff]="$PYTHON_RUFF"
511	            [python-mypy]="$PYTHON_MYPY"
512	            [python-interrogate]="$PYTHON_INTERROGATE"
513	            [python-xenon]="$PYTHON_XENON"
514	            [python-security]="$PYTHON_SECURITY"
515	            [dead-code]="$DEAD_CODE"
516	          )
517	          LANES=(biome deps circular file-size types-location components-per-file \
518	                 duplicates type-coverage package-hygiene type-check \
519	                 python-ruff python-mypy python-interrogate python-xenon python-security \
520	                 dead-code)
521	
522	          # ---------------------------------------------------------------
523	          # RATCHET: a lane is ENFORCED (can fail this gate) iff the marker
524	          # file .github/quality-gate/enforced/<lane> exists. Every other
525	          # lane is reported but never blocks merge. Each resolution PR adds
526	          # its own marker file — separate paths never merge-conflict the way
527	          # editing one shared list does. When all 15 markers exist, the full
528	          # strict rule set is enforced.
529	          # ---------------------------------------------------------------
530	          ENFORCED=()
531	          if [ -d .github/quality-gate/enforced ]; then
532	            for marker in .github/quality-gate/enforced/*; do
533	              [ -e "$marker" ] || continue
534	              ENFORCED+=("$(basename "$marker")")
535	            done
536	          fi
537	          # ---------------------------------------------------------------
538	
539	          is_enforced() { local l; for l in "${ENFORCED[@]}"; do [[ "$l" == "$1" ]] && return 0; done; return 1; }
540	
541	          echo "::group::Per-lane results"
542	          for lane in "${LANES[@]}"; do
543	            if is_enforced "$lane"; then tag="[enforced]"; else tag="[informational]"; fi
544	            printf '  %-22s %-12s %s\n' "$lane:" "${RESULT[$lane]}" "$tag"
545	          done
546	          echo "::endgroup::"
547	
548	          failed=0
549	          for lane in "${ENFORCED[@]}"; do
550	            r="${RESULT[$lane]}"
551	            if [[ "$r" != "success" ]]; then
552	              echo "::error::enforced lane '$lane' did not pass (result: $r)"
553	              failed=$((failed + 1))
554	            fi
555	          done
556	
557	          if [[ "$failed" -gt 0 ]]; then
558	            echo "::error::Quality gate FAILED — $failed enforced lane(s) did not pass"
559	            exit 1
560	          fi
561	          echo "Quality gate: PASSED — ${#ENFORCED[@]}/${#LANES[@]} lanes enforced; remainder informational (ratchet in progress)"
562

file.content:
name: Code Quality

# Tech-debt prevention gates that run on every PR to develop or master.
# Each lane is independent so failures are isolated and parallelised. The
# `quality-gate` job at the bottom is the single required check.
#
# Output convention for every step:
#   1. The step name is the plain-English explanation of WHAT is being checked.
#   2. The script body wraps the raw tool output in ::group::/::endgroup::
#      collapsibles so reviewers see a pass/fail summary line first.
#   3. The final echo is a one-line human + LLM readable verdict.
#
# Companion to .github/workflows/main.yml — that workflow handles
# correctness (build, type-check, tests). This workflow enforces code
# hygiene to prevent low-quality / LLM-generated code from accumulating.
on:
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - develop
      - master
  push:
    branches:
      - develop
      - master
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: code-quality-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.head_ref || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  # --- Biome lint + format -------------------------------------------------
  biome:
    name: Biome lint + format
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
      - name: Lint and format check across all TS/JS/JSON/CSS files
        run: |
          echo "::group::Biome check"
          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh ts tsx js jsx json jsonc css)
          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
            pnpm run quality:lint
          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
          else
            pnpm exec biome check $FILES
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "Biome: OK (0 errors; warnings shown above are non-blocking)"

  # --- Workspace dependency hygiene ---------------------------------------
  deps:
    name: Workspace dependency hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
      - name: All workspace packages agree on dep versions (syncpack)
        run: |
          echo "::group::syncpack lint"
          pnpm exec syncpack lint
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "syncpack: OK (dep versions and semver ranges consistent)"
      - name: Workspace topology is valid (manypkg)
        run: |
          echo "::group::manypkg check"
          pnpm exec manypkg check
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "manypkg: OK (workspace structure valid)"
      - name: package.json shape conforms (npm-package-json-lint)
        run: |
          echo "::group::package.json lint"
          pnpm run quality:pkg-json
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "pkg-json: OK (all package.json files conform to schema)"

  # --- Circular dependency check ------------------------------------------
  circular:
    name: No circular imports
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
      - name: Circular dependency scan (madge)
        run: |
          echo "::group::madge --circular"
          pnpm run quality:circular
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "Circular deps: OK (no cycles found)"

  # --- File size limit (per-extension + allowlist + hard cap) -------------
  file-size:
    name: File size limits
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
      - name: Per-file line count (default 400, relaxed 700, hard cap 1200)
        run: |
          echo "::group::File size scan"
          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh ts tsx js jsx mjs cjs)
          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
            pnpm run quality:size
          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
          else
            CHANGED_FILES="$FILES" node scripts/ci/check-file-sizes.mjs
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "File size: OK (no hard-cap violations)"

  # --- Types must live in dedicated type files ----------------------------
  types-location:
    name: Types live in dedicated *.types.ts files
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
      - name: Type / interface / enum location enforcer
        run: |
          echo "::group::types-location check"
          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh ts tsx)
          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
            node scripts/ci/check-types-location.mjs --enforce
          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
          else
            CHANGED_FILES="$FILES" node scripts/ci/check-types-location.mjs --enforce
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "Type colocation: OK (no file exports > 3 types unless in *.types.ts)"

  # --- Components-per-file ------------------------------------------------
  components-per-file:
    name: At most 2 React components per file
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
      - name: Components-per-file enforcer
        run: |
          echo "::group::components-per-file check"
          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh tsx)
          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
            pnpm run quality:components
          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
          else
            CHANGED_FILES="$FILES" node scripts/ci/check-components-per-file.mjs
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "Components: OK (no file declares > 2 React components)"

  # --- Copy-paste detection -----------------------------------------------
  duplicates:
    name: Copy-paste detection (jscpd)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
      - name: Duplicate code detection (threshold in .jscpd.json)
        run: |
          echo "::group::jscpd scan"
          pnpm run quality:dupe
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "Duplicates: OK (under threshold)"

  # --- Type coverage ------------------------------------------------------
  type-coverage:
    name: Explicit-type coverage per workspace
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
      - name: Type-coverage (98-99% per workspace)
        run: |
          echo "::group::type-coverage per workspace"
          pnpm run quality:type-coverage
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "Type coverage: OK (every workspace meets its threshold)"

  # --- Published package hygiene ------------------------------------------
  package-hygiene:
    name: Published package hygiene (publint + attw)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
      - name: publint — validate packages/cli npm metadata
        run: |
          echo "::group::publint packages/cli"
          pnpm run quality:publint
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "publint: OK (packages/cli npm metadata is valid)"
      - name: are-the-types-wrong — validate type exports
        run: |
          echo "::group::attw packages/cli"
          OUTPUT=$(pnpm run quality:attw 2>&1)
          echo "$OUTPUT"
          if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "This package does not contain types"; then
            echo "::endgroup::"
            echo "attw: OK (packages/cli is a CLI binary — no exported types is expected)"
          else
            echo "::endgroup::"
            echo "attw: OK (type exports resolve across CJS/ESM/node16/bundler)"
          fi

  # --- TypeScript strict type-check ---------------------------------------
  type-check:
    name: TypeScript strict type-check (all workspaces)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
      - name: tsc --noEmit across web, desktop, mobile, cli, bots
        run: |
          echo "::group::nx type-check"
          if [ -n "${GITHUB_BASE_REF:-}" ]; then
            git fetch --no-tags --depth=1 origin "$GITHUB_BASE_REF" 2>/dev/null || true
            pnpm exec nx affected -t type-check --base=origin/$GITHUB_BASE_REF
          else
            pnpm exec nx run-many -t type-check --projects=web,desktop,mobile,cli,bot-discord,bot-slack,bot-telegram,bot-whatsapp
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "TypeScript: OK (all affected workspaces pass strict tsc)"

  # --- Python ruff --------------------------------------------------------
  python-ruff:
    name: Python ruff (strict rule set)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@d4b2f3b6ecc6e67c4457f6d3e41ec42d3d0fcb86 # v5.4.2
        with:
          version: "latest"
          enable-cache: true
      - name: ruff check + format (PL, RUF, FAST, S, B, A, COM, C4, DTZ, ISC, RET, SIM, ARG, PTH, TRY, PERF, UP, FURB, LOG, G + more)
        run: |
          echo "::group::ruff check + format --check"
          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh py)
          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
            uvx ruff check apps/api apps/voice-agent libs/shared/py
            uvx ruff format --check apps/api apps/voice-agent libs/shared/py
          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
          else
            uvx ruff check $FILES
            uvx ruff format --check $FILES
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "ruff: OK (strict rule set passes; every file is already formatted)"

  # --- Python mypy --------------------------------------------------------
  python-mypy:
    name: Python mypy (staged-strict)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 20
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@d4b2f3b6ecc6e67c4457f6d3e41ec42d3d0fcb86 # v5.4.2
        with:
          version: "latest"
          enable-cache: true
      - name: Install api dev + backend dependencies (uv sync)
        run: |
          echo "::group::uv sync"
          uv sync --project apps/api --group backend --group dev
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "deps: OK"
      - name: mypy across api + voice-agent + shared (staged-strict + shared.* strict override)
        run: |
          echo "::group::mypy"
          # mypy needs the whole module graph per project, so it can't be scoped
          # to individual changed files. On PRs we instead detect which Python
          # projects changed (from the .py diff) and check only those project
          # roots. libs/shared/py is a dependency of both apps, so any change
          # there pulls both apps in. On push we check everything.
          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh py)
          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
            uv run --project apps/api --group backend --group dev mypy apps/api/app apps/voice-agent/src libs/shared/py --ignore-missing-imports
          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
            echo "No changed Python files — skipping."
          else
            TARGETS=""
            if echo "$FILES" | grep -q '^libs/shared/py/'; then
              TARGETS="apps/api/app apps/voice-agent/src libs/shared/py"
            else
              echo "$FILES" | grep -q '^apps/api/'         && TARGETS="$TARGETS apps/api/app"
              echo "$FILES" | grep -q '^apps/voice-agent/' && TARGETS="$TARGETS apps/voice-agent/src"
            fi
            if [ -z "$TARGETS" ]; then
              echo "No tracked Python project changed — skipping."
            else
              uv run --project apps/api --group backend --group dev mypy $TARGETS --ignore-missing-imports
            fi
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "mypy: OK (no type errors)"

  # --- Python docstring coverage ------------------------------------------
  python-interrogate:
    name: Python docstring coverage (interrogate ≥ 80%)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@d4b2f3b6ecc6e67c4457f6d3e41ec42d3d0fcb86 # v5.4.2
        with:
          version: "latest"
          enable-cache: true
      - name: Docstring coverage on public API surface
        run: |
          echo "::group::interrogate"
          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh py)
          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
            uvx interrogate -c pyproject.toml apps/api apps/voice-agent libs/shared/py
          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
          else
            uvx interrogate -c pyproject.toml $FILES
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "Docstrings: OK (coverage ≥ 80%)"

  # --- Python cyclomatic complexity ---------------------------------------
  python-xenon:
    name: Python cyclomatic complexity (xenon)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@d4b2f3b6ecc6e67c4457f6d3e41ec42d3d0fcb86 # v5.4.2
        with:
          version: "latest"
          enable-cache: true
      - name: Complexity scan (max-absolute F, max-modules E, max-average B)
        run: |
          echo "::group::xenon"
          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh py)
          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
            uvx xenon \
              --max-absolute F \
              --max-modules E \
              --max-average B \
              apps/api apps/voice-agent libs/shared/py \
              -e 'apps/api/tests/**' \
              -e 'apps/api/scripts/**' \
              -e 'libs/shared/py/tests/**'
          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
          else
            uvx xenon \
              --max-absolute F \
              --max-modules E \
              --max-average B \
              $FILES
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "Complexity: OK (no module exceeds rank D, codebase average ≤ B)"

  # --- Python security (bandit gates; pip-audit advisory) -----------------
  python-security:
    name: Python security (bandit; pip-audit advisory)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@d4b2f3b6ecc6e67c4457f6d3e41ec42d3d0fcb86 # v5.4.2
        with:
          version: "latest"
          enable-cache: true
      - name: Bandit security scan (zero-warning policy)
        run: |
          echo "::group::bandit (low severity, low confidence — no exclusions)"
          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh py)
          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
            uvx bandit -c pyproject.toml -r apps/api/app apps/voice-agent/src libs/shared/py --severity-level low --confidence-level low
          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
          else
            uvx bandit -c pyproject.toml $FILES --severity-level low --confidence-level low
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "Security: OK (0 issues across all severity levels)"
      - name: pip-audit (dependency CVEs)
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          echo "::group::pip-audit dependency scan"
          uvx pip-audit --strict --skip-editable || true
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "pip-audit: informational — review CVEs above (does not fail CI)"

  # --- Dead code detection (knip + vulture) — enforced ---------------------
  # Runs in --strict mode: any knip (TS) or vulture (Python) finding fails the
  # lane. The scan is tightened to drop systematic false positives (tests,
  # Pydantic/dynamic-dispatch, framework-consumed files) — see knip.config.ts
  # and apps/api/scripts/vulture-whitelist.py.
  dead-code:
    name: Dead code (knip + vulture, strict)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@d4b2f3b6ecc6e67c4457f6d3e41ec42d3d0fcb86 # v5.4.2
        with:
          version: "latest"
          enable-cache: true
      - name: Install vulture (Python dead-code detector)
        run: |
          echo "::group::uv tool install vulture"
          uv tool install vulture
          echo "::endgroup::"
      - name: knip (TypeScript) + vulture (Python) dead-code scan (strict)
        run: |
          echo "::group::dead-code scan"
          bash scripts/dead-code-check.sh --strict --verbose
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "Dead code: OK (no unused TS or Python code)"

  # --- Quality gate (single required status check) -------------------------
  quality-gate:
    name: Quality gate (required)
    if: always()
    needs:
      - biome
      - deps
      - circular
      - file-size
      - types-location
      - components-per-file
      - duplicates
      - type-coverage
      - package-hygiene
      - type-check
      - python-ruff
      - python-mypy
      - python-interrogate
      - python-xenon
      - python-security
      - dead-code
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 2
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Verdict
        env:
          # Lane results passed through env to avoid inline-expansion injection.
          BIOME: ${{ needs.biome.result }}
          DEPS: ${{ needs.deps.result }}
          CIRCULAR: ${{ needs.circular.result }}
          FILE_SIZE: ${{ needs.file-size.result }}
          TYPES_LOCATION: ${{ needs.types-location.result }}
          COMPONENTS_PER_FILE: ${{ needs.components-per-file.result }}
          DUPLICATES: ${{ needs.duplicates.result }}
          TYPE_COVERAGE: ${{ needs.type-coverage.result }}
          PACKAGE_HYGIENE: ${{ needs.package-hygiene.result }}
          TYPE_CHECK: ${{ needs.type-check.result }}
          PYTHON_RUFF: ${{ needs.python-ruff.result }}
          PYTHON_MYPY: ${{ needs.python-mypy.result }}
          PYTHON_INTERROGATE: ${{ needs.python-interrogate.result }}
          PYTHON_XENON: ${{ needs.python-xenon.result }}
          PYTHON_SECURITY: ${{ needs.python-security.result }}
          DEAD_CODE: ${{ needs.dead-code.result }}
        run: |
          declare -A RESULT=(
            [biome]="$BIOME"
            [deps]="$DEPS"
            [circular]="$CIRCULAR"
            [file-size]="$FILE_SIZE"
            [types-location]="$TYPES_LOCATION"
            [components-per-file]="$COMPONENTS_PER_FILE"
            [duplicates]="$DUPLICATES"
            [type-coverage]="$TYPE_COVERAGE"
            [package-hygiene]="$PACKAGE_HYGIENE"
            [type-check]="$TYPE_CHECK"
            [python-ruff]="$PYTHON_RUFF"
            [python-mypy]="$PYTHON_MYPY"
            [python-interrogate]="$PYTHON_INTERROGATE"
            [python-xenon]="$PYTHON_XENON"
            [python-security]="$PYTHON_SECURITY"
            [dead-code]="$DEAD_CODE"
          )
          LANES=(biome deps circular file-size types-location components-per-file \
                 duplicates type-coverage package-hygiene type-check \
                 python-ruff python-mypy python-interrogate python-xenon python-security \
                 dead-code)

          # ---------------------------------------------------------------
          # RATCHET: a lane is ENFORCED (can fail this gate) iff the marker
          # file .github/quality-gate/enforced/<lane> exists. Every other
          # lane is reported but never blocks merge. Each resolution PR adds
          # its own marker file — separate paths never merge-conflict the way
          # editing one shared list does. When all 15 markers exist, the full
          # strict rule set is enforced.
          # ---------------------------------------------------------------
          ENFORCED=()
          if [ -d .github/quality-gate/enforced ]; then
            for marker in .github/quality-gate/enforced/*; do
              [ -e "$marker" ] || continue
              ENFORCED+=("$(basename "$marker")")
            done
          fi
          # ---------------------------------------------------------------

          is_enforced() { local l; for l in "${ENFORCED[@]}"; do [[ "$l" == "$1" ]] && return 0; done; return 1; }

          echo "::group::Per-lane results"
          for lane in "${LANES[@]}"; do
            if is_enforced "$lane"; then tag="[enforced]"; else tag="[informational]"; fi
            printf '  %-22s %-12s %s\n' "$lane:" "${RESULT[$lane]}" "$tag"
          done
          echo "::endgroup::"

          failed=0
          for lane in "${ENFORCED[@]}"; do
            r="${RESULT[$lane]}"
            if [[ "$r" != "success" ]]; then
              echo "::error::enforced lane '$lane' did not pass (result: $r)"
              failed=$((failed + 1))
            fi
          done

          if [[ "$failed" -gt 0 ]]; then
            echo "::error::Quality gate FAILED — $failed enforced lane(s) did not pass"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "Quality gate: PASSED — ${#ENFORCED[@]}/${#LANES[@]} lanes enforced; remainder informational (ratchet in progress)"

content.text:
Now I have develop's current workflow (#704 "hybrid scoping"). Two root causes are confirmed:

1. **`type-check` lane (lines 240-245)**: on PRs it runs `nx affected -t type-check` with **no project filter**. Since this PR touches `apps/api`, nx pulls in the `api` project — whose `type-check` target is `uv run … mypy` — but the node-only job has no `uv` → `uv: not found`. This is a bug in develop's #704 workflow: the affected variant forgot to scope to `tag:scope:typescript` (the non-PR branch correctly lists only TS projects).
2. **`python-interrogate` lane (lines 343-350)**: on PRs it runs `interrogate` on the changed `.py` files only → the PR's new code is at 63% < 80%.

Let me confirm the git relationship cleanly from the primary repo, and verify the type-check bug is general (hits other Python PRs too).

input.command: cd /Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia && echo "=== merge-base branch vs origin/develop ===" && git merge-base feat/whatsapp-media-support origin/develop 2>&1 | grep -v zoxide; echo "=== ahead/behind (left=branch ahead, right=develop ahead) ===" && git rev-list --left-right --count feat/whatsapp-media-support...origin/develop 2>&1 | grep -v zoxide; echo "=== did branch ever modify code-quality.yml? ===" && git log --oneline feat/whatsapp-media-support -- .github/workflows/code-quality.yml 2>&1 | grep -v zoxide | head -3; echo "(if empty, branch never touched it)"
input.description: Clean git relationship check from primary repo

content.text: The git diagnostics show something alarming: **no merge-base** between the branch and local `origin/develop`, with a `2531 / 2` split — that's *disjoint history*, which can't be right for a feature branch. Yet the branch's own history clearly contains develop's quality commits (#692–#694), and GitHub successfully ran develop's #704 workflow on the PR (so GitHub thinks they're mergeable). My **local `origin/develop` ref is corrupted** (likely from my earlier fetches). Let me get the true remote state.

input.command: cd /Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia && echo "=== remote url ===" && git config remote.origin.url 2>&1 | grep -v zoxide; echo "=== TRUE remote develop sha ===" && git ls-remote origin develop 2>&1 | grep -v zoxide; echo "=== local origin/develop sha ===" && git rev-parse origin/develop 2>&1 | grep -v zoxide; echo "=== commit count on local origin/develop ===" && git rev-list --count origin/develop 2>&1 | grep -v zoxide; echo "=== is true-develop an ancestor relationship? merge-base branch vs local develop(d2b6ccbc) ===" && git merge-base feat/whatsapp-media-support develop 2>&1 | grep -v zoxide

content.text: The picture is now clear, and it explains the contradictions: my local `origin/develop` is **shallow** (only 2 commits fetched: #704 and #701), which is why merge-base came back empty. The true remote develop tip is `186ffbef` (#704), and that **#704 workflow is exactly the buggy one** — it's the tip, so there's no newer fix waiting. Let me confirm the shallow state and the enforced-lane facts, then I'll lay out the plan.

content.text:
Diagnosis complete and confirmed empirically. Here's the real picture:

## Both CI failures are bugs in develop's just-merged #704 "hybrid scoping" workflow — not in your PR's feature code

**1. `type-check` lane** (`code-quality.yml` line 242): on PRs it runs `nx affected -t type-check` with **no project filter**. Your PR touches `apps/api`, so nx pulls in the `api` project — whose `type-check` target is `uv run … mypy` — but the node-only job has no `uv` → `uv: not found`. The non-PR branch of that same lane correctly lists only TS projects; the `affected` variant just forgot to scope to `tag:scope:typescript`. **This breaks every PR that touches Python.**

**2. `python-interrogate` lane** (line 349): on PRs it passes the explicit changed `$FILES` to interrogate. I confirmed live that **explicit paths bypass the `exclude` config** in `pyproject.toml` (which is supposed to drop `**/tests/**`, `**/scripts/**`, etc.):
- Passing a test file explicitly → counted at **17.9%** (should've been skipped).
- Your changed **source** files (tests/scripts excluded) actually score **~81%** → would pass.
- With tests wrongly included → **63%** → fails.

(The same `$FILES` pattern likely mis-scopes `python-ruff`, `python-xenon`, and `python-security` too.) SonarCloud is a separate third-party gate I'll look at after the required gate is green.

Both `type-check` and `python-interrogate` are **enforced** lanes, so both block the required "Quality gate."

Since the root cause is shared CI infra (and you appear to own this quality-gate system, per the #692–#704 commits), I want your call on where the fix lands before I touch anything — the type-check fix *must* live in `code-quality.yml` (there's no clean branch-local workaround).

Two more notes: your working dir `gaia-onboarding-revamp` is broken orphaned cruft (merged onboarding PR; not a git repo) — I've been working in the `gaia-feat-whatsapp-media-support` worktree. And the branch is ~9 commits behind develop.

=== scripts/ci/changed-files.sh (origin/develop) ===
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# changed-files.sh — emit the list of changed files for a CI lane to scope to.
#
# Usage:
#   scripts/ci/changed-files.sh <ext> [<ext> ...]
#   e.g. scripts/ci/changed-files.sh ts tsx js jsx json css
#        scripts/ci/changed-files.sh py
#
# Modes (signalled to callers via a sentinel on the first line of stdout):
#
#   PUSH / FULL-SCAN MODE  ($GITHUB_BASE_REF is empty — i.e. not a PR)
#     Prints the single line "__FULL__" and exits 0. Callers MUST treat this
#     as "scan the whole repo with the lane's existing full command".
#
#   PR MODE  ($GITHUB_BASE_REF is set — pull_request event)
#     Prints one path per line: files changed vs the PR base (merge-base diff),
#     filtered to the requested extensions and to files that still exist on
#     HEAD (added / copied / modified / renamed; deletions excluded). When the
#     PR changes zero matching files this prints NOTHING and exits 0 — callers
#     MUST treat empty (but not "__FULL__") output as "no work, skip & pass".
#
# Caller contract (the three states):
#   FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh <exts>)
#   if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then  <full command>          # push
#   elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then          echo "skip"; exit 0     # PR, nothing relevant
#   else                                <tool> $FILES           # PR, diff-scoped
#   fi
#
# Diff accuracy note: the merge-base ("...") diff requires the base ref to be
# present locally, so PR lanes that consume this script must checkout with
# `fetch-depth: 0`. The script fetches the base ref defensively as well.
set -euo pipefail

FULL_SENTINEL="__FULL__"

# Push / non-PR event: no base ref to diff against → signal full scan.
if [[ -z "${GITHUB_BASE_REF:-}" ]]; then
  printf '%s\n' "$FULL_SENTINEL"
  exit 0
fi

if [[ "$#" -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "changed-files.sh: at least one extension argument is required" >&2
  exit 2
fi

# Build an alternation of the requested extensions: ts|tsx|js → \.(ts|tsx|js)$
ext_alt=""
for ext in "$@"; do
  if [[ -z "$ext_alt" ]]; then
    ext_alt="$ext"
  else
    ext_alt="$ext_alt|$ext"
  fi
done
ext_regex="\.(${ext_alt})$"

# Ensure the PR base ref is available for the merge-base diff. Best-effort:
# on a fetch-depth:0 checkout this is a cheap no-op; on a shallow one it
# unshallows just enough to resolve the base.
git fetch --no-tags --depth=1 origin "$GITHUB_BASE_REF" 2>/dev/null || true

# `...HEAD` diffs against the merge-base of the base ref and HEAD — the same set
# of files GitHub shows as "Files changed" in the PR. --diff-filter=ACMR drops
# deletions so we never hand a tool a path that no longer exists.
# `|| true` on grep: a PR that changes zero matching files is a valid "skip"
# case, not an error. Without it, grep's no-match exit 1 + `set -o pipefail`
# would make this script exit 1 and fail the lane's `FILES=$(...)` step.
git diff --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR "origin/${GITHUB_BASE_REF}...HEAD" \
  | { grep -E "$ext_regex" || true; } \
  | while IFS= read -r f; do
      [[ -f "$f" ]] && printf '%s\n' "$f"
    done

  | while IFS= read -r f; do
      [[ -f "$f" ]] && printf '%s\n' "$f"
    done

file.content:
# --- TypeScript strict type-check ---------------------------------------
  type-check:
    name: TypeScript strict type-check (all workspaces)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-node-pnpm
      - name: tsc --noEmit across web, desktop, mobile, cli, bots
        run: |
          echo "::group::nx type-check"
          if [ -n "${GITHUB_BASE_REF:-}" ]; then
            git fetch --no-tags --depth=1 origin "$GITHUB_BASE_REF" 2>/dev/null || true
            pnpm exec nx affected -t type-check --base=origin/$GITHUB_BASE_REF
          else
            pnpm exec nx run-many -t type-check --projects=web,desktop,mobile,cli,bot-discord,bot-slack,bot-telegram,bot-whatsapp
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "TypeScript: OK (all affected workspaces pass strict tsc)"

  # --- Python ruff --------------------------------------------------------
  python-ruff:
    name: Python ruff (strict rule set)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

content.text: Now I'll make the type-check fix — scope the affected run to TypeScript projects only (mirroring `main.yml`'s `tag:scope:typescript` pattern), so the Python `api` project no longer gets pulled into this node-only job.

input.old_string:
echo "::group::nx type-check"
          if [ -n "${GITHUB_BASE_REF:-}" ]; then
            git fetch --no-tags --depth=1 origin "$GITHUB_BASE_REF" 2>/dev/null || true
            pnpm exec nx affected -t type-check --base=origin/$GITHUB_BASE_REF
          else
            pnpm exec nx run-many -t type-check --projects=web,desktop,mobile,cli,bot-discord,bot-slack,bot-telegram,bot-whatsapp
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "TypeScript: OK (all affected workspaces pass strict tsc)"
input.new_string:
echo "::group::nx type-check"
          if [ -n "${GITHUB_BASE_REF:-}" ]; then
            git fetch --no-tags --depth=1 origin "$GITHUB_BASE_REF" 2>/dev/null || true
            # Scope to affected *TypeScript* projects only. A bare
            # `nx affected -t type-check` also matches Python projects — api has
            # a mypy-backed type-check target — which fail in this node-only job
            # with "uv: not found". tag:scope:typescript mirrors the explicit
            # project list in the push/full-scan branch below; this is the same
            # affected+tag pattern main.yml uses to split the TS and Python lanes.
            TS_PROJECTS=$(pnpm exec nx show projects --affected --base="origin/$GITHUB_BASE_REF" --projects 'tag:scope:typescript' 2>/dev/null | paste -sd, -)
            if [ -n "$TS_PROJECTS" ]; then
              pnpm exec nx run-many -t type-check --projects="$TS_PROJECTS"
            else
              echo "No affected TypeScript projects — skipping."
            fi
          else
            pnpm exec nx run-many -t type-check --projects=web,desktop,mobile,cli,bot-discord,bot-slack,bot-telegram,bot-whatsapp
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "TypeScript: OK (all affected workspaces pass strict tsc)"

echo "mypy: OK (no type errors)"

  # --- Python docstring coverage ------------------------------------------
  python-interrogate:
    name: Python docstring coverage (interrogate ≥ 80%)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@d4b2f3b6ecc6e67c4457f6d3e41ec42d3d0fcb86 # v5.4.2
        with:
          version: "latest"
          enable-cache: true
      - name: Docstring coverage on public API surface
        run: |
          echo "::group::interrogate"
          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh py)
          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
            uvx interrogate -c pyproject.toml apps/api apps/voice-agent libs/shared/py
          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
          else
            uvx interrogate -c pyproject.toml $FILES
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "Docstrings: OK (coverage ≥ 80%)"

  # --- Python cyclomatic complexity ---------------------------------------
  python-xenon:
    name: Python cyclomatic complexity (xenon)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@d4b2f3b6ecc6e67c4457f6d3e41ec42d3d0fcb86 # v5.4.2
        with:
          version: "latest"
          enable-cache: true
      - name: Complexity scan (max-absolute F, max-modules E, max-average B)
        run: |
          echo "::group::xenon"
          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh py)
          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
            uvx xenon \
              --max-absolute F \
              --max-modules E \
              --max-average B \
              apps/api apps/voice-agent libs/shared/py \
              -e 'apps/api/tests/**' \
              -e 'apps/api/scripts/**' \
              -e 'libs/shared/py/tests/**'
          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
          else
            uvx xenon \
              --max-absolute F \
              --max-modules E \
              --max-average B \
              $FILES
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"
          echo "Complexity: OK (no module exceeds rank D, codebase average ≤ B)"

  # --- Python security (bandit gates; pip-audit advisory) -----------------
  python-security:
    name: Python security (bandit; pip-audit advisory)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@d4b2f3b6ecc6e67c4457f6d3e41ec42d3d0fcb86 # v5.4.2
        with:
          version: "latest"
          enable-cache: true
      - name: Bandit security scan (zero-warning policy)
        run: |
          echo "::group::bandit (low severity, low confidence — no exclusions)"
          FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh py)
          if [ "$FILES" = "__FULL__" ]; then
            uvx bandit -c pyproject.toml -r apps/api/app apps/voice-agent/src libs/shared/py --severity-level low --confidence-level low
          elif [ -z "$FILES" ]; then
            echo "No matching changed files — skipping."
          else
            uvx bandit -c pyproject.toml $FILES --severity-level low --confidence-level low

content.text: Now let me create a shared helper that emits changed Python **source** files (excluding the paths the tools skip in full-scan mode), and wire interrogate, xenon, and bandit to use it.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# changed-py-source.sh — changed Python *source* files for PR-scoped lanes.
#
# Thin wrapper over changed-files.sh that drops the non-source paths the Python
# analysis tools skip during a full-scan directory traversal (tests, scripts,
# migrations, alembic, conftest.py, setup.py).
#
# WHY: when a lane passes explicit file paths to a tool, the tool's own
# directory-scan excludes are bypassed — e.g. `interrogate -c pyproject.toml
# path/to/test_x.py` checks that file even though pyproject's
# [tool.interrogate].exclude lists "**/tests/**". That makes a PR's verdict
# diverge from the equivalent full scan (a changed test file tanks docstring
# coverage). Filtering the file list here restores parity.
#
# Mirrors changed-files.sh's three-state contract exactly so callers are
# unchanged apart from the script name:
#   __FULL__  → push / full-scan event (passed through untouched)
#   (empty)   → PR with no matching source files (caller skips & passes)
#   <list>    → PR: newline-separated source files
#
# NOTE: ruff is intentionally NOT a consumer — its full-scan command lints the
# whole tree including tests, so raw changed-files.sh py is the correct scope
# there. mypy resolves to project roots, so it is unaffected too.
set -euo pipefail

FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh py)

# Pass the full-scan sentinel and the empty (skip) case through unchanged.
if [[ "$FILES" == "__FULL__" || -z "$FILES" ]]; then
  printf '%s\n' "$FILES"
  exit 0
fi

# Keep in sync with [tool.interrogate].exclude in pyproject.toml and the
# xenon -e / bandit path selection in code-quality.yml. Each pattern matches a
# whole path segment (anchored to start-of-string or a slash) so substrings
# like "latest_tests" or "scripts_helper.py" are not falsely excluded.
printf '%s\n' "$FILES" \
  | { grep -vE '(^|/)(tests|scripts|migrations|alembic)/|(^|/)(conftest|setup)\.py$' || true; }

ci(quality): scope PR lanes — TS-only type-check, source-only python scans

#704's hybrid (diff-on-PR) scoping broke two enforced lanes for any PR that
touches Python:

- type-check ran `nx affected -t type-check` with no project filter, pulling
  the Python `api` project (mypy-backed type-check target) into the node-only
  job → "uv: not found". Scope the affected run to tag:scope:typescript — the
  same affected+tag split main.yml already uses to separate the TS and Python
  type-check lanes.

- interrogate/xenon/bandit passed explicit changed $FILES to the tool, which
  bypasses each tool's directory-scan excludes — a changed test file then
  counts toward docstring coverage / complexity / security and can fail the
  lane. Add scripts/ci/changed-py-source.sh to drop tests, scripts, migrations,
  alembic, conftest.py and setup.py from the changed-file list, restoring
  parity with full-scan. ruff (intentionally lints tests) and mypy (resolves to
  project roots) keep the raw changed-file list.

EOF

content.text: Pushed. Now let me open the PR against develop.

input.command:
cd /Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-ci-quality-affected-scoping && gh pr create --base develop --head ci/quality-affected-scoping --title "ci(quality): scope PR lanes — TS-only type-check, source-only python scans" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## What

Fixes two enforced Code Quality lanes that #704's hybrid (diff-on-PR) scoping broke for **any PR that touches Python**.

### 1. `type-check` pulled the Python `api` project into the node-only job
On PRs the lane ran `nx affected -t type-check` with no project filter. Since `api` has a mypy-backed `type-check` target, nx ran it in the TypeScript job — which has no `uv`/Python toolchain — so it died with `/bin/sh: 1: uv: not found`.

Fix: scope the affected run to `tag:scope:typescript` (api/voice-agent/shared-python are `scope:python`). This is the **same affected+tag split `main.yml` already uses** to separate the TS and Python type-check lanes, and matches the explicit project list in this lane's push/full-scan branch.

### 2. Python changed-file scans bypassed their excludes
`interrogate`, `xenon`, and `bandit` passed the explicit changed `$FILES` to the tool. Explicit paths **bypass each tool's directory-scan excludes** (`pyproject` `[tool.interrogate].exclude`, xenon `-e`, bandit's `app/`/`src/`-only roots), so a changed **test** file got counted toward docstring coverage/complexity/security and could fail the lane even though a full scan would skip it. (Confirmed: passing a test file to interrogate explicitly scored it at 17.9% instead of skipping.)

Fix: add `scripts/ci/changed-py-source.sh`, a thin wrapper over `changed-files.sh py` that drops `tests/`, `scripts/`, `migrations/`, `alembic/`, `conftest.py`, and `setup.py` — restoring parity with full-scan. `ruff` (intentionally lints tests) and `mypy` (resolves to project roots) keep the raw changed-file list.

## Why it matters
Both lanes are **enforced**, so every PR touching `apps/api` Python currently fails the required Quality gate on a CI-config bug, not on its own code. (Surfaced by PR #688.)

## Verification
- `nx show projects --projects 'tag:scope:typescript'` → api/voice-agent/shared-python excluded.
- Filter regex unit-checked: keeps `app/foo.py`, `scripts_helper.py`, `latest_tests/y.py`; drops `tests/…`, `scripts/…`, `conftest.py`, `alembic/…`, `setup.py`.
- `bash -n` on the helper + YAML parse both pass; helper preserves the `__FULL__` / empty / list three-state contract.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | grep -vE "zoxide|ajeetdsouza|Disable this|consider filing|Please ensure" | tail -10

content.text:
#707 is **green on the required check**. Confirmed working:

- ✅ **Quality gate (required): PASS**
- ✅ **TypeScript strict type-check: PASS** (the `api`/`uv: not found` bug is fixed)
- ✅ **Python docstring coverage (interrogate): PASS**
- ✅ **SonarCloud: PASS**

(Only non-required `Workers Builds` / `CodeRabbit` are still pending.) The workflow fix is valid and doesn't break any lane.

---

## Summary

**The "CI fails" on PR #688 was not caused by #688's code — it was two bugs in develop's just-merged #704 "hybrid scoping" CI workflow, which break *every* PR that touches Python.**

**What I did:**

1. **Diagnosed** the two failing enforced lanes:
   - `type-check`: ran `nx affected -t type-check` with no project filter → pulled the Python `api` project (mypy target) into the node-only job → `uv: not found`.
   - `python-interrogate`: passed explicit changed `$FILES` to interrogate, which **bypasses the `exclude` config** → test files got counted, dragging coverage to 63%.

2. **Fixed it at the source → [PR #707](https://github.com/theexperiencecompany/gaia/pull/707)** off develop (per your choice):
   - Scoped the affected type-check to `tag:scope:typescript` (mirrors `main.yml`).
   - Added `scripts/ci/changed-py-source.sh` so interrogate/xenon/bandit drop tests/scripts/migrations/etc., matching full-scan. ruff (intentionally lints tests) and mypy (root-based) untouched.
   - **#707's own CI is green**, validating the fix.

3. **Docstrings — turned out to be unnecessary.** Despite the CI table showing `bot.py` at 50% etc., that was scanning a *stale, divergent merge-commit* (develop has a larger `bot.py`; note the table's `TestBatchSyncConversations` at L847 and mismatched line numbers). The branch's actual code is **already 100% documented** — every public function in `bot.py`, `rate_limiting.py`, `bot_models.py`, `agent.py` has a docstring. I did **not** add redundant ones.

4. **SonarCloud is not a required check** (only `quality-gate` is) — it doesn't block merge, and it passes on #707 anyway.

## What's left for #688 to go green

There's nothing more to change *on #688 itself* — the fix lives in develop via #707:

1. A human merges **#707 → develop** (I don't merge PRs).
2. **#688 syncs develop** (merge/rebase) to pick up the fixed workflow → its `quality-gate` passes.

⚠️ Heads-up for that sync: #688 is **9 commits behind develop and has a divergent `bot.py`** (develop's is larger) — expect a non-trivial conflict to resolve.

**Want me to merge develop into #688 now** to resolve that `bot.py` divergence ahead of time? It won't turn CI green until #707 lands on develop, and it needs unshallowing the repo + careful conflict resolution — so I'd rather confirm before doing it. Otherwise I'll leave #688 as-is until #707 is merged.

(Also: the directory you launched from, `gaia-onboarding-revamp`, is broken orphaned cruft from the merged onboarding PR — not a git repo. Happy to clean it up if you want.)

content.text:
`voice-agent` and `shared-python` have **no** `type-check` target — so `api` is the only Python project nx would run in that lane. That means your `--exclude=api,wake-word` in #688 is actually **complete and correct** for type-check (and intentionally omits `wake-word`, which my `tag:scope:typescript` would wrongly re-include). When #688 merges, develop gets that correct type-check fix.

So #707's type-check change is now **redundant** (and slightly worse). The only thing #707 uniquely fixes at the develop level is the **interrogate/xenon/bandit bug** where explicit changed-`$FILES` bypass the `pyproject` excludes — you worked around it in #688 by documenting the test files, but develop still mis-counts test files for *every other* PR.

## Where things stand
- ✅ **#688 fully green** (your `03c518f1d`) — task resolved.
- 🔵 **#707** open, but its type-check half is now redundant with your in-branch fix.

How do you want to handle #707?

          if [ -n "${GITHUB_BASE_REF:-}" ]; then
            git fetch --no-tags --depth=1 origin "$GITHUB_BASE_REF" 2>/dev/null || true
            # Scope to affected *TypeScript* projects only. A bare
            # `nx affected -t type-check` also matches Python projects — api has

            TS_PROJECTS=$(pnpm exec nx show projects --affected --base="origin/$GITHUB_BASE_REF" --projects 'tag:scope:typescript' 2>/dev/null | paste -sd, -)

              pnpm exec nx run-many -t type-check --projects="$TS_PROJECTS"
            else
              echo "No affected TypeScript projects — skipping."
            fi
          else
            pnpm exec nx run-many -t type-check --projects=web,desktop,mobile,cli,bot-discord,bot-slack,bot-telegram,bot-whatsapp

          echo "TypeScript: OK (all affected workspaces pass strict tsc)"

## What

Fixes a scoping bug introduced by #704's hybrid (diff-on-PR) Code Quality scoping, for the **Python analysis lanes** (`interrogate`, `xenon`, `bandit`).

On PRs those lanes pass the explicit changed `$FILES` to the tool. **Explicit paths bypass each tool's directory-scan excludes** (`pyproject` `[tool.interrogate].exclude`, xenon `-e`, bandit's `app/`/`src/` roots), so a changed **test** or **script** file gets analyzed even though a full scan would skip it. For interrogate this means a changed test file counts toward docstring coverage and can fail the lane (confirmed: a test file passed explicitly scored 17.9% instead of being skipped).

## Fix

Add `scripts/ci/changed-py-source.sh` — a thin wrapper over `changed-files.sh py` that drops `tests/`, `scripts/`, `migrations/`, `alembic/`, `conftest.py`, and `setup.py`, restoring parity with full-scan. Wire `interrogate`/`xenon`/`bandit` to it.

- `ruff` is intentionally **not** a consumer — its full-scan lints the whole tree including tests, so the raw changed-file list is correct there.
- `mypy` resolves to project roots, so it's unaffected.
- The **type-check** lane's separate `uv: not found` bug is fixed by #688 (`--exclude=api,wake-word`), so it's **left untouched here** to avoid overlapping that change.

## Verification
- Filter regex unit-checked: keeps `app/foo.py`, `scripts_helper.py`, `latest_tests/y.py`; drops `tests/…`, `scripts/…`, `conftest.py`, `alembic/…`, `setup.py`.
- `bash -n` + YAML parse pass; helper preserves the `__FULL__` / empty / list three-state contract of `changed-files.sh`.

message.content: # Keep in sync with [tool.interrogate].exclude in pyproject.toml and the# xenon -e / bandit path selection in code-quality.yml. Each pattern matches a# whole path segment (anchored to start-of-string or a slash) so substrings# like "latest_tests" or "scripts_helper.py" are not falsely excluded.printf '%s\n' "$FILES" \  | { grep -vE '(^|/)(tests|scripts|migrations|alembic)/|(^|/)(conftest|setup)\.py$' || true; } so this needs to be kept in sync manualyl or??

content.text:
Yes — as written, it's **manual sync**, and you've spotted the real weakness. The grep hardcodes the exclude segments, so if someone later edits `[tool.interrogate].exclude` in `pyproject.toml` (adds a new excluded dir, renames one), the helper silently drifts. The dangerous direction is silent: a stale helper that *keeps* excluding something `pyproject` no longer does would quietly drop real source files from the scan, and nobody notices because the lane still goes green.

The xenon `-e` and bandit roots are a different story — those genuinely live in `code-quality.yml` with no shared source, so there's no single list to point at. But the one that actually broke (interrogate) *does* have a canonical list in `pyproject.toml`.

So the better design: **make the helper read the excludes from `pyproject.toml`** instead of hardcoding them — one source of truth, nothing to sync. All the current patterns reduce to either "a path segment named X" (`**/tests`, `**/scripts`, `**/alembic`, …) or "a basename X.py" (`**/conftest.py`, `**/setup.py`), so a short Python block can derive them:

```bash
printf '%s\n' "$FILES" | python3 - <<'PY'
import sys, tomllib, pathlib
pats = tomllib.loads(pathlib.Path("pyproject.toml").read_text())["tool"]["interrogate"]["exclude"]
segs, bases = set(), set()
for p in pats:
    core = p.strip("/")
    core = core[3:] if core.startswith("**/") else core
    core = core[:-3] if core.endswith("/**") else core
    core = core.strip("/")
    if "/" in core or "*" in core:        # shape we don't model → don't exclude (fail loud, not silent)
        continue
    (bases if core.endswith(".py") else segs).add(core)
for line in filter(None, sys.stdin.read().splitlines()):
    parts = line.split("/")
    if not (segs & set(parts) or parts[-1] in bases):
        print(line)
PY
```

`tomllib` is stdlib (runner has Python 3.12), so no new deps. xenon/bandit then ride on interrogate's canonical list — documented, and it's the strictest superset anyway, so it's a safe approximation rather than a second hardcoded copy.

Trade-off: it's ~15 lines of embedded Python vs. a one-line grep. If you'd rather keep the helper dead-simple, the grep+comment is fine too — the list is stable and the comment flags it.

Want me to swap #707 over to the `pyproject`-driven version?

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# changed-py-source.sh — changed Python *source* files for PR-scoped lanes.
#
# Thin wrapper over changed-files.sh that drops the non-source paths the Python
# analysis tools skip during a full-scan directory traversal (tests, scripts,
# migrations, alembic, conftest.py, setup.py, virtualenvs).
#
# WHY: when a lane passes explicit file paths to a tool, the tool's own
# directory-scan excludes are bypassed — e.g. `interrogate -c pyproject.toml
# path/to/test_x.py` checks that file even though pyproject's
# [tool.interrogate].exclude lists "**/tests/**". That makes a PR's verdict
# diverge from the equivalent full scan (a changed test file tanks docstring
# coverage). Filtering the file list here restores parity.
#
# The exclude list is read straight from [tool.interrogate].exclude in
# pyproject.toml — that is the single source of truth, so there is nothing to
# keep in sync by hand. interrogate is the strictest consumer; xenon and bandit
# ride on the same list (a safe superset of their own narrower excludes).
#
# Mirrors changed-files.sh's three-state contract exactly so callers are
# unchanged apart from the script name:
#   __FULL__  → push / full-scan event (passed through untouched)
#   (empty)   → PR with no matching source files (caller skips & passes)
#   <list>    → PR: newline-separated source files
#
# NOTE: ruff is intentionally NOT a consumer — its full-scan command lints the
# whole tree including tests, so raw changed-files.sh py is the correct scope
# there. mypy resolves to project roots, so it is unaffected too.
set -euo pipefail

FILES=$(scripts/ci/changed-files.sh py)

# Pass the full-scan sentinel and the empty (skip) case through unchanged.
if [[ "$FILES" == "__FULL__" || -z "$FILES" ]]; then
  printf '%s\n' "$FILES"
  exit 0
fi

# Drop the excluded paths, deriving the patterns from pyproject so this never
# drifts from interrogate's own config. Every current pattern reduces to either
# a path *segment* (**/tests, **/scripts, .venv, …) or a *basename*
# (**/conftest.py, **/setup.py); anything with a shape we don't model is left
# in (errs toward a louder lane, never a silent drop). tomllib is stdlib on the
# runner's Python 3.11+.
printf '%s\n' "$FILES" | python3 - <<'PY'
import pathlib
import sys
import tomllib

patterns = tomllib.loads(pathlib.Path("pyproject.toml").read_text())[
    "tool"
]["interrogate"]["exclude"]

segments, basenames = set(), set()
for pat in patterns:
    core = pat.strip("/")
    if core.startswith("**/"):
        core = core[3:]
    if core.endswith("/**"):
        core = core[:-3]
    core = core.strip("/")
    if "/" in core or "*" in core:  # shape we don't model -> don't exclude
        continue
    (basenames if core.endswith(".py") else segments).add(core)

for line in filter(None, sys.stdin.read().splitlines()):
    parts = line.split("/")
    if segments & set(parts) or parts[-1] in basenames:
        continue
    print(line)
PY
